## api-build: Build the API server binary
api-build:
	$(MAKE) -C api build

## build-full: Build single binary with embedded web frontend (API + SPA)
build-full: web-build
	$(MAKE) -C api build-full

## api-run: Run the API server
api-run:
	$(MAKE) -C api run

## api-generate: Run GraphQL code generation
api-generate:
	$(MAKE) -C api generate

## api-test: Run all API tests
api-test:
	$(MAKE) -C api test

## api-test-unit: Run API unit tests
api-test-unit:
	$(MAKE) -C api test-unit

## api-lint: Run linter on the API
api-lint:
	$(MAKE) -C api lint

## api-migrate-up: Apply database migrations
api-migrate-up:
	$(MAKE) -C api migrate-up

## api-migrate-down: Roll back last database migration
api-migrate-down:
	$(MAKE) -C api migrate-down

## api-clean: Remove API build artifacts
api-clean:
	$(MAKE) -C api clean

## schema-gen: Generate common/schema/schema.sql from migration Up blocks
schema-gen:
	bash scripts/gen-schema.sh

## web-install: Install web frontend dependencies
web-install:
	$(MAKE) -C web install

## web-dev: Start Vite dev server (port 8080, proxies /graphql → :8081)
web-dev:
	$(MAKE) -C web dev

## web-build: Type-check and build web frontend for production
web-build:
	$(MAKE) -C web build

## web-lint: Run ESLint on web frontend
web-lint:
	$(MAKE) -C web lint

## web-clean: Remove web build artifacts and node_modules
web-clean:
	$(MAKE) -C web clean

## web-test: Run web unit tests
web-test:
	$(MAKE) -C web test

## web-test-coverage: Run web unit tests with coverage report
web-test-coverage:
	$(MAKE) -C web test-coverage

## web-e2e: Run Playwright E2E tests for web frontend (starts Vite dev server automatically)
web-e2e:
	$(MAKE) -C web e2e-test

## web-test-e2e: Run all Playwright E2E tests (alias with webServer auto-start)
web-test-e2e:
	$(MAKE) -C web e2e-test

## web-test-e2e-critical: Run only @critical tier E2E tests
web-test-e2e-critical:
	$(MAKE) -C web e2e-test-critical

## web-screenshots: Capture visual baseline screenshots across viewports
web-screenshots:
	$(MAKE) -C web screenshots

## web-storybook: Start Storybook dev server for UI components (port 6006)
web-storybook:
	$(MAKE) -C web storybook

## web-schema-sync: Regenerate web/schema.graphql and graphql-env.d.ts from API SDL files
web-schema-sync:
	cat api/graph/schema/schema.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/auth.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/data_sources.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/echo.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/field_names_gen.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/institution.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/preferences.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/search.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/search_fields.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/similarity.graphql \
	    api/graph/schema/zip.graphql \
	    > web/schema.graphql
	cd web && pnpm exec gql-tada generate-output --tsconfig tsconfig.app.json

## lint: Run all linters (Go + web)
lint: api-lint web-lint

## audit: Run security audit on web dependencies (non-zero exit on high+ severity)
audit:
	cd web && pnpm audit --audit-level=high

## hadolint: Lint all Dockerfiles with hadolint (runs via Docker)
hadolint:
	@find . -name 'Dockerfile*' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -not -path '*/dist/*' | sort | \
		while read -r f; do \
			echo "Linting $$f"; \
			docker run --rm -i hadolint/hadolint < "$$f" || exit 1; \
		done

## smoke-test: Run search smoke test against localhost:8081 (requires running API)
smoke-test:
	bash scripts/smoke-test.sh

## smoke-test-with-server: Build API, start it, run smoke test, tear down (requires DATABASE_URL)
smoke-test-with-server:
	bash scripts/smoke-test-with-server.sh

## api-image-build: Build the standalone API production Docker image
api-image-build:
	docker build -f api/Dockerfile \
		--build-arg VERSION=$$(git describe --tags --always --dirty 2>/dev/null || echo dev) \
		--build-arg BUILD_TIME=$$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
		-t kapow-api \
		api/

## web-image-build: Build the standalone web frontend Docker image (nginx + Vite build)
web-image-build:
	docker build \
		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_URL=$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL") \
		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY") \
		-t kapow-web web/

## test-image-build: Build the all-in-one test Docker image (postgres+API+nginx)
test-image-build: hadolint
	docker build \
		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_URL=$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL") \
		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY") \
		-t kapow-test .

## test-image-run: Run the test image, exposing the web UI on http://localhost:8080
test-image-run: test-image-build
	docker run --rm -p 8080:80 kapow-test

## demo: Build test image, run it, and launch the interactive Playwright demo
demo:
	bash scripts/demo.sh $(DEMO_ARGS)

## dev: Start full-stack dev environment (PostgreSQL + API + web) with hot reload
dev:
	bash scripts/dev.sh

## dev-clean: Remove this checkout's dev database volume (forces fresh fixture load on next `make dev`)
dev-clean:
	@HASH=$$(echo "$$PWD" | md5sum | cut -c1-8); \
	VOL="kapow-dev-pgdata-$$HASH"; \
	CONT="kapow-dev-$$HASH"; \
	docker rm -f "$$CONT" 2>/dev/null || true; \
	if docker volume inspect "$$VOL" >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
		docker volume rm "$$VOL"; \
		echo "Removed volume $$VOL"; \
	else \
		echo "Volume $$VOL does not exist"; \
	fi

## dev-db-up: Start standalone dev database (PostgreSQL+PostGIS on port 5433, full dataset)
dev-db-up:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml up -d --wait
	@echo ""
	@echo "Dev database starting on port 5433. Use:"
	@echo "  DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/kapow?sslmode=disable"

## dev-db-down: Stop and remove the standalone dev database container
dev-db-down:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml down

## compose-up: Start 3-service production topology (db + api + web) on http://localhost:8080
compose-up:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml up -d --build --wait

## compose-down: Stop and remove compose services and volumes
compose-down:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml down -v

## compose-logs: Tail logs from all compose services
compose-logs:
	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml logs -f

## dev-prune: Remove kapow-dev volumes older than 30 days
dev-prune:
	@CUTOFF=$$(date -d '30 days ago' +%s 2>/dev/null || date -v-30d +%s); \
	for vol in $$(docker volume ls --filter label=kapow-dev=true -q 2>/dev/null); do \
		CREATED=$$(docker volume inspect "$$vol" --format '{{.CreatedAt}}' 2>/dev/null); \
		VOL_TS=$$(date -d "$$CREATED" +%s 2>/dev/null || date -j -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S" "$$CREATED" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0); \
		if [ "$$VOL_TS" -lt "$$CUTOFF" ]; then \
			echo "Removing old volume: $$vol (created $$CREATED)"; \
			docker volume rm "$$vol" 2>/dev/null || true; \
		fi; \
	done; \
	echo "Prune complete."

## kapow-build: Build the kapow data pipeline binary
kapow-build:
	cd tools/kapow && go build -o ../../bin/kapow .

## kapow-test: Run all kapow tool tests
kapow-test:
	cd tools/kapow && go test ./...

## kapow-test-unit: Run kapow unit tests only (no DB)
kapow-test-unit:
	cd tools/kapow && go test -short ./...

## tools-build: Build all tools (samplesql, kapow)
tools-build: kapow-build
	cd tools/samplesql && go build -o ../../bin/samplesql .

## tools-test: Run tests for all tools
tools-test: kapow-test
	cd tools/samplesql && go test ./...

## test-quick: Fast checks — Go unit tests, TS type-check + build, ESLint (~10-20s, no DB)
test-quick:
	MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh api-test-unit web-build web-lint

## test-standard: Full local validation — quick + web unit tests + Go lint (~30-60s, no DB)
test-standard:
	$(MAKE) test-quick && MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh web-test api-lint

## test-fixture: Validate fixture data loads cleanly against current schema (spins up temp Docker PG)
test-fixture:
	bash scripts/test-fixture.sh

## test-full: Complete validation — standard + integration, e2e, smoke, fixture (requires DB + running services)
test-full:
	$(MAKE) test-standard && MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh api-test test-fixture tools-test && $(MAKE) web-test-e2e && $(MAKE) smoke-test

## fixture-sql: Generate complete.sql.xz from JSON fixture data (requires Docker)
fixture-sql:
	bash scripts/gen-fixture-sql.sh

## fixture-json: Re-extract JSON fixture data from a live database (requires DATABASE_URL)
fixture-json:
	cd tools/samplesql && go run . --format json --output-dir ../../common/src/main/resources/data/tables

## spec-gen: Generate reference documentation in docs/specs/generated/
spec-gen:
	bash scripts/gen-specs.sh

## fixture-sql: Generate complete.sql.xz from JSON fixture data (requires Docker)
fixture-sql:
	bash scripts/gen-fixture-sql.sh

## fixture-json: Re-extract JSON fixture data from a live database (requires DATABASE_URL)
fixture-json:
	cd tools/samplesql && go run . --format json --output-dir ../../common/src/main/resources/data/tables

## help: List all available targets
help:
	@ESSENTIAL="api-generate build-full dev help lint test-image-run test-full test-quick test-standard web-schema-sync"; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "Usage: make <target>"; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "Essential targets:"; \
	echo ""; \
	grep -hE '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
			for e in $$ESSENTIAL; do \
				if [ "$$target" = "$$e" ]; then \
					printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
					break; \
				fi; \
			done; \
		done; \
	echo ""; \
	echo "All targets:"; \
	echo ""; \
	grep -hE '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
			SKIP=false; \
			for e in $$ESSENTIAL; do \
				if [ "$$target" = "$$e" ]; then SKIP=true; break; fi; \
			done; \
			if [ "$$SKIP" = "false" ]; then \
				printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
			fi; \
		done

- `worktree/json-fixtures` — JSON fixture system replacing COPY-format SQL (resolved 2 trivial conflicts in `.gitignore` and `Makefile`)

**Deleted (already on main):**
- `worktree/map-view` — feature was already merged via a different commit

**Cleanup totals:**
- 6 stale local branches deleted
- 27 remote branches deleted (23 merged + 4 just processed)
- 1 worktree removed
- Final state: just `main`, no worktrees, no stale branches

Base directory for this skill: /home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/skills/swarm-project

# Swarm Project: Kapow

Delegates to the global `/swarm` workflow with Kapow-specific configuration
injected. Run `/swarm-project` exactly as you would `/swarm`.

## Usage

- `/swarm-project` — work all `bd ready` issues
- `/swarm-project 3` — max 3 concurrent teammates
- `/swarm-project 3 id-abc id-def` — specific issues, max 3 concurrent

## Instructions

Run the global `/swarm` skill, but override Phase 0 discovery with the
project config below. Do NOT read `.claude/swarm-config.md` — everything
is already here.

---

## Project Config (replaces Phase 0)

### Quality Gates (per teammate)

Choose based on files changed:

| Changed | Command |
|---|---|
| Go only | `make api-test-unit && make api-lint` |
| Web only | `cd web && pnpm build && pnpm lint && pnpm test` |
| Both | `make test-standard` |
| GraphQL schema | Run `make api-generate && make web-schema-sync` **before** other gates |

### Full Quality Gate (Phase 5, on main)

```bash
make test-standard
```

### Post-Merge Hooks

If the merged branch touched any `graph/schema/*.graphql` file:

```bash
make api-generate && make web-schema-sync
```

If the demo walkthrough (`demo/steps.ts`) references changed UI, update it.

### Branch Naming

`worktree/<agent-name>` — use the teammate's name, not the issue ID.

### Git Workflow

Merge directly to `main` from the command line. Do not create pull requests.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /home/ketan/.claude/skills/swarm

# Swarm: Parallel Issue Work

Work `bd ready` issues in parallel using agent teams. Each teammate works in an
isolated worktree with plan-mode supervision.

## Usage

- `/swarm` — work all `bd ready` issues (max 8 concurrent teammates)
- `/swarm 3` — work `bd ready` issues, max 3 concurrent teammates
- `/swarm 3 id-abc id-def id-ghi` — specific issues, max 3 concurrent

## Batch Size Limit

A single swarm run processes **at most 20 tasks**. Managing more than 20 tasks
in one context window degrades quality. If the total eligible issue count exceeds
20, the swarm works the first 20 (by priority), then hands off the remainder
via the continuation protocol below.

---

## Phase 0 — Discover Project Config

Look for `.claude/swarm-config.md` in the project root. If it exists, read it
and use its values for the phases below. It may define any of:

- **Quality gate commands** — what to run before declaring work complete
- **Pre-completion skill** — a project skill teammates must run before reporting done
- **Post-merge hooks** — commands to run on main after merging a branch
- **Branch naming** — convention for worktree branch names (default: `<issue-id>`)

If the file does not exist, use these defaults:
- Quality gates: whatever the project's CLAUDE.md describes for running tests/lints
- Pre-completion: none (teammates just report done with quality gate results)
- Post-merge hooks: none
- Branch naming: `<issue-id>`

---

## Phase 1 — Triage

**Parse arguments**: The first argument, if numeric, is the max concurrent teammates.
Remaining arguments are issue IDs. If no numeric first argument, concurrency defaults to 8.

**Check for continuation file**: If `.claude/swarm-continue.txt` exists and no
issue IDs were passed as arguments, read the IDs and concurrency from the file,
delete it, and use those IDs as the input set (skip `bd ready`).

1. Run `bd ready` (or use the provided issue IDs, or the continuation file)
2. **Skip epics** — epics are containers, not units of work. Filter out any issue
   with `[epic]` in the title or `type: epic`.
3. Run `bd show <id>` for each non-epic issue to understand scope
4. Identify issues that touch **overlapping files** — these must be sequenced, not parallelized
5. Skip issues that are too large or ambiguous for a single teammate (flag for manual work)
6. **Apply batch limit**: If eligible issues exceed **20**, take the top 20 by
   priority (P0 first, then dependency order). The remainder become the
   **overflow set** — they will be worked in a subsequent `/swarm` invocation
   after this batch completes (see Phase 7).
7. Of the issues in this batch (≤20), if they exceed the concurrency cap
   (default 8), prioritize by beads priority and dependency order. Remaining
   issues go into a **wait queue** and will be spawned as active teammates
   complete (see Phase 4 — Backfill on Completion).
8. Present the triage plan to the user:
   - Which issues will be parallelized (batch 1)
   - Which issues are in the wait queue (if limited by concurrency)
   - Which issues overflow to the next batch (if limited by the 20-task cap)
   - Which issues are sequenced and why
   - Which issues are skipped and why

**Wait for user approval before proceeding.**

---

## Phase 2 — Team Setup

1. Create a team via `TeamCreate` (name: `swarm-YYYY-MM-DD` or similar)
2. Create a task (via `TaskCreate`) for each parallelizable issue, including the
   beads ID in the task description
3. For each task, spawn a teammate via the `Agent` tool with:
   - `subagent_type: "general-purpose"`
   - `mode: "plan"`
   - `team_name: <team>`
   - Do NOT use `isolation: "worktree"` — it is unreliable. Instead, include
     manual worktree setup commands in the prompt (see below).
4. Each teammate's prompt must include:
   - The full issue details (from `bd show`)
   - Instruction to follow CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md conventions
   - The quality gate commands from swarm-config (or CLAUDE.md)
   - If swarm-config defines a pre-completion skill, instruction to run it and
     include the output in the completion message
   - **Manual worktree setup** (copy verbatim into each prompt, substituting
     the branch name per the naming convention):
     ```
     ## WORKTREE SETUP — RUN FIRST
     Before doing anything else, create and enter an isolated worktree:
     ```bash
     REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
     BRANCH_NAME="<branch>"
     WORKTREE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/$BRANCH_NAME"

     if [ -d "$WORKTREE_DIR" ]; then
       echo "Worktree already exists, reusing"
     else
       git worktree add "$WORKTREE_DIR" -b "$BRANCH_NAME"
     fi

     cd "$WORKTREE_DIR"
     ```
     Verify you are in the right place:
     ```bash
     echo "CWD: $(pwd)"
     echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)"
     echo "$(pwd)" | grep -q '\.claude/worktrees/' && echo "WORKTREE VERIFIED" || echo "FATAL: NOT in a worktree"
     ```
     If verification fails, STOP and report the error to the team lead.

     ALL subsequent commands must run from this worktree directory.
     NEVER cd back to the main repo directory. Commit and push ONLY on
     your worktree branch — never touch main.

     When done, push your branch:
     ```bash
     git push -u origin HEAD
     ```
     ```

---

## Phase 3 — Plan Review

For each teammate that submits a plan:

1. Review the plan for:
   - Correct scope (matches the issue, no scope creep)
   - Quality gates included in the plan
   - Test coverage plan (tests are comprehensive and well-targeted)
   - No overlap with other teammates' work
   - Working directory references `.claude/worktrees/` (not the main repo)
2. Approve or reject via `plan_approval_response`
3. If rejecting, provide specific feedback on what to change

---

## Phase 4 — Monitor & Merge

As teammates complete work:

1. **Verify quality**: Confirm the teammate ran the required quality gates and
   they passed. If a pre-completion skill is configured, confirm the teammate
   included its output and it shows PASS. Reject any completion that doesn't
   meet these criteria — send the teammate back to fix.
2. **Get the branch**: The branch name follows the naming convention from Phase 2
3. **Merge to main**:
   ```bash
   git checkout main
   git merge <branch> --no-edit
   ```
4. **Handle conflicts**: If merge conflicts arise, resolve them or ask the teammate to rebase
5. **Post-merge hooks**: If swarm-config defines post-merge commands, run them now
6. **Close the beads issue**: `bd close <id>`
7. **Clean up worktree and branch**:
   ```bash
   git worktree remove .claude/worktrees/<branch>
   git branch -d <branch>
   ```
8. **Only then** send `shutdown_request` to the teammate

**CRITICAL**: Never shut down a teammate before merging their branch. Always
merge first, then clean up, then shut down.

### Backfill on Completion

Each time a teammate completes and is merged+shut down, if the number of
active teammates is below the concurrency cap and issues remain in the wait
queue:

1. Re-evaluate the wait queue: pick the highest-priority issue (P0 first,
   then beads priority order) whose files do **not** overlap with any
   currently in-flight teammate's work.
2. If a non-conflicting issue is found, spawn a new teammate for it
   (following Phase 2 setup).
3. If **all** remaining wait-queue issues conflict with in-flight work,
   do **not** spawn — wait until more teammates finish and re-evaluate.
4. Repeat until the wait queue is empty or all remaining issues conflict.

---

## Phase 5 — Quality Gates

After **all** teammates are merged:

1. Run the project's full quality gate on main (per swarm-config or CLAUDE.md)
2. If any failures, fix them on main directly
3. If swarm-config defines additional post-merge validation (e.g., a review skill
   or integration check), run it now

---

## Phase 6 — Close Protocol

1. Close any remaining beads issues: `bd close <id1> <id2> ...`
2. Sync and push:
   ```bash
   bd sync
   git pull --rebase
   git push
   git status  # Must show "up to date with origin"
   ```
3. Shut down all teammates (if not already done)
4. Delete the team via `TeamDelete`
5. Report summary to user:
   - Issues completed
   - Issues skipped or deferred
   - Any quality gate findings
   - Final git status
6. **Continuation**: If issues remain from the batch overflow (see Phase 1),
   proceed to Phase 7.

---

## Phase 7 — Batch Continuation

If more than 20 eligible issues were identified in Phase 1, only the first 20
are worked in Phases 2–6. After Phase 6 completes:

1. **Save remaining issue IDs** to `.claude/swarm-continue.txt` (one ID per line)
2. **Report to the user**:
   ```
   Batch 1 complete: <N> issues done, <M> issues remaining.
   Remaining issues saved to .claude/swarm-continue.txt.
   Invoking /swarm to continue with next batch...
   ```
3. **Invoke `/swarm`** with the remaining issue IDs (read from the continue file).
   This starts a fresh context window. The new invocation will:
   - Read `.claude/swarm-continue.txt` if it exists and no explicit issue IDs
     are provided
   - Delete the file after reading it
   - Work up to the next 20 issues, repeating until all are done

### Continue File Format

`.claude/swarm-continue.txt`:
```
# Swarm continuation — remaining issues from batch started YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM
# Concurrency: <N>
id-aaa
id-bbb
id-ccc
```

Phase 1 must check for this file at startup. If it exists and no issue IDs were
passed as arguments:
1. Read the issue IDs and concurrency from the file
2. Delete the file
3. Use those IDs as the input set (skip the `bd ready` call)

===== kapow-94e =====

Owner: Ketan Gangatirkar · Type: task
Created: 2026-03-09 · Updated: 2026-03-09

DESCRIPTION
`kapow run-all` — fetch all + import all in dependency order.

Orchestration:
- Layer 0 (no deps): nces, zipcoords, urban (fetch + import)

===== kapow-hft =====
○ kapow-hft · Implement ZIP coordinates importer   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: Ketan Gangatirkar · Type: task
Created: 2026-03-09 · Updated: 2026-03-09

DESCRIPTION
Package: tools/importer/zipcoords/

Fetch side (`kapow fetch zipcoords`):
- Download CSV from GeoNames or similar public source (ZIP, lat, lon, municipality, state)
- Archive to data/downloads/

Import side (`kapow import zipcoords`):
- Parse CSV
- Bulk insert into location.zip_coordinates with PostGIS geometry (ST_SetSRID(ST_MakePoint(lon,lat),4326))
- Uses CopyFrom for performance

===== kapow-xtd =====
○ kapow-xtd · Implement Köppen climate importer   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: Ketan Gangatirkar · Type: task
Created: 2026-03-09 · Updated: 2026-03-09

DESCRIPTION
Package: tools/importer/weather/

Fetch side (`kapow fetch weather`):
- Download Köppen-Geiger ASCII text file (public domain)
- Archive to data/downloads/

Import side (`kapow import weather`):
- Parse ASCII grid (lat, lon, classification)
- grid.go — normalize institution coordinates to 0.25/0.75 grid, spiral search up to 3 degrees for nearest match
- Write institution.weather JSONB + WEATHER dataset

No Urban Institute equivalent — this is a unique data source.

Unit test for grid/spiral logic; integration test.


===== kapow-60e =====
○ kapow-60e · Implement NCES Field of Study importer   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: Ketan Gangatirkar · Type: task
Created: 2026-03-09 · Updated: 2026-03-09

DESCRIPTION
Package: tools/importer/ncesfos/

Fetch side:
- `kapow fetch nces-fos` downloads FieldOfStudyData CSV from collegescorecard.ed.gov/data/, archives to data/downloads/

Import side (`kapow import nces-fos`):
- Parse FieldOfStudyData CSV from archive
- Map credential level codes (1=CERT, 2=ASSOC, 3=BACH, 4=MAST, 5=DOCT, 6=PROF)
- Group by unit_id + CIP code
- Uses canonicalUnitId() for OPE normalization
- Write to institution.fields_of_study JSONB
- Insert into search_base.cip_data

Unit test with fixture; integration test.


===== kapow-b2n =====
○ kapow-b2n · Implement internal/ranking builder   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: Ketan Gangatirkar · Type: task
Created: 2026-03-09 · Updated: 2026-03-09

DESCRIPTION
Package: tools/importer/internal/ranking/

Shared infrastructure for all 9+ ranking importers.

Files:
- builder.go — Opinion struct (publication/category slugs, method rank/score/tier/range, year, unit_id, rank, score, tier). RankingBuilder accumulates opinions, Flush(ctx) batch-writes:
  1. Upsert ranking metadata into search_base.ranking
  2. Upsert into search_base.annual_dataset (theme=RANKING)
  3. Merge into institution.rankings JSONB
- deriveOpinion() — parse numeric ranks, tier strings ("Tier 1"), and ranges ("201-300")

Unit tests for opinion derivation; integration test for flush.

Done when: Integration test flushes 10 opinions → ranking + annual_dataset + institution.rankings populated.


===== kapow-u8c =====
○ kapow-u8c · Import university hex brand colors from frishberg/University-Hex-Colors   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: Ketan Gangatirkar · Type: task
Created: 2026-03-09 · Updated: 2026-03-09

DESCRIPTION
Source: https://github.com/frishberg/University-Hex-Colors/blob/main/data.json

2,639 universities with hex brand colors (e.g. "Alabama A & M University": "660000"). Keyed by institution name (no IPEDS/unit_id — needs name matching).

Fetch side:
- `kapow fetch hex-colors` downloads data.json from GitHub, archives to data/downloads/

Import side (`kapow import hex-colors`):
- Parse JSON (simple dict: name → hex string)
- Match names to institutions via namematch Tier 1
- Store in institution.misc JSONB as brand_color field (e.g. {"brand_color": "#660000"})
- Log unmatched names for review

Could be used alongside favicons for visual identity in search results and detail views.


===== kapow-vdg =====
○ kapow-vdg · Mock authentication provider for local development   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: Ketan Gangatirkar · Type: task
Created: 2026-03-09 · Updated: 2026-03-09

DESCRIPTION
## Problem

Local development and E2E testing require either a full Supabase setup (credentials, OAuth configuration) or manual JWT generation. This creates friction for:

- New developers running `make dev` for the first time
- E2E tests that need authenticated sessions
- The interactive demo walkthrough

## Proposed solution

Add a dev-only mock auth provider that issues valid JWTs without Supabase.

### API side
- New endpoint (e.g. `/dev/auth/login`) guarded by `ENV == development`
- Accepts email, name, and sub (user ID) parameters; returns a signed JWT using the configured `JWT_SECRET`

Type: task
Created: 2026-03-09 · Updated: 2026-03-09

DESCRIPTION
- Priority: 2
- Labels: data, bug
Filter out non-degree-granting satellite campuses, certificate programs, and community colleges. Apply a permanent WHERE clause filtering out institutions with `nces->>'degrees_awarded_highest'` in (0, 1, 2):
- 0 = Non-degree-granting (342 institutions — satellite/partnership locations like ASU-Pima)
- 1 = Certificate only (2,026 — trade schools)
- 2 = Associate's only (1,298 — community colleges)
Only show institutions with `degrees_awarded_highest` >= 3 (Bachelor's or Graduate). This filter is unconditional — not exposed as a facet in the UI or API. Apply at the database/query layer so it affects all search paths.

- **With search query**: sort by relevance (full-text rank)
- **With both**: sort by relevance, distance as tiebreaker
- **Neither (just browsing)**: sort alphabetically

===== kapow-ibx =====
○ kapow-ibx · Implement Admissions platform importer   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: Ketan Gangatirkar · Type: task
Created: 2026-03-09 · Updated: 2026-03-09

DESCRIPTION
Package: tools/importer/admissions/

Fetch side:
- Embedded membership lists (no download needed) or periodically updated from organization websites

Import side (`kapow import admissions`):
- platforms.go — embedded membership lists for:
  - Common App (~1,500 members)
  - Coalition (~150 members)
  - ApplyTexas
  - Apply SUNY
  - UC system
  - CSU system

===== kapow-rki =====
○ kapow-rki · Implement Crime/Clery Act importer   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: Ketan Gangatirkar · Type: task
Created: 2026-03-09 · Updated: 2026-03-09

DESCRIPTION
Package: tools/importer/crime/

Note: Urban Institute API (kapow-edj) provides campus-crime/hate-crimes endpoint which may cover this. This importer handles the fuller Clery Act dataset from OPE if the Urban API is insufficient.

Fetch side (`kapow fetch crime`):
- Primary: use Urban Institute API campus-crime endpoint (already JSON, unitid-keyed)
- Fallback: download XLS from https://ope.ed.gov/campussafety/#/datafile/list, convert to CSV via libreoffice
- Archive to data/downloads/

Import side (`kapow import crime`):
- Parse multiple CSVs or Urban API JSON (on-campus, non-campus, VAWA categories)
- Deduplicate by OPE8 preferring Main Campus
- Aggregate crime categories (murder, rape, robbery, etc.) and VAWA categories (dating violence, stalking)
- Write institution.safety_and_security JSONB + SAFETY dataset
- Document XLS→CSV conversion step if needed

===== kapow-e01 =====
○ kapow-e01 · Implement namematch Tier 2 (fuzzy matching)   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: Ketan Gangatirkar · Type: task
Created: 2026-03-09 · Updated: 2026-03-09

DESCRIPTION
Package: tools/importer/internal/namematch/fuzzy.go

Jaro-Winkler fuzzy string matching for names that Tier 1 (deterministic) can't resolve. Expected to cover ~3-4% more matches.

- Uses adrg/strutil for Jaro-Winkler similarity
- When Tier 1 fails, compute similarity against all institutions (pre-filtered by state when available)
- Match threshold: 0.92
- Return top candidates with confidence scores
- Log unmatched names + fuzzy candidates to a JSON report file for review

Unit test: fuzzy match for misspelled institution name.

Done when: Unit test shows fuzzy match; report file generated.

Priority: P3

===== kapow-nmd =====
○ kapow-nmd · Implement match-review subcommand   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: Ketan Gangatirkar · Type: task
Created: 2026-03-09 · Updated: 2026-03-09

DESCRIPTION
`kapow match-review` — interactive review of unmatched institution names.

- Load unmatched name report from last import run
- Display each unmatched name with top fuzzy candidates
- --approve ID persists a match as new alias in search_base.institution_alias
- --reject ID marks as known non-match (skip in future runs)
- Track review state in local JSON file

Done when: Can review unmatched names and persist aliases.

Priority: P4
Deps: kapow-fuzzy (Tier 2 fuzzy matching)


===== kapow-h29 =====
○ kapow-h29 · Implement namematch Tier 3 (LLM-assisted)   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: Ketan Gangatirkar · Type: task
Created: 2026-03-09 · Updated: 2026-03-09

DESCRIPTION
Package: tools/importer/internal/namematch/llm.go

LLM-assisted name matching for the long tail that Tier 1+2 can't resolve.

- --llm-assist flag on match-review subcommand
- Batch unmatched names with context (source publication, state, ranking position, top fuzzy candidates)
- Send to Claude API for suggested matches
- Uses anthropic-sdk-go
- Present LLM suggestions alongside fuzzy candidates in match-review
- All matches still require human approval

Done when: LLM suggestions appear in match-review for unresolved names.

Priority: P4
Deps: match-review subcommand

===== kapow-edj =====
○ kapow-edj · Urban Institute Education Data Portal integration   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: Ketan Gangatirkar · Type: task
Created: 2026-03-09 · Updated: 2026-03-09

DESCRIPTION
The Urban Institute Education Data Portal (https://educationdata.urban.org/) provides a free REST API with JSON responses, keyed by IPEDS unitid (same as our unit_id). No authentication required. Paginated JSON responses.

Base URL: https://educationdata.urban.org/api/v1/college-university/

This single API replaces or supplements many planned data sources. Pull ALL available data — we'll sort out what to surface later.

### Datasets to fetch (all years available)

**IPEDS (core institutional data):**
- /ipeds/directory/{year}/ — name, address, state, URLs, Carnegie class (1980–2024)
- /ipeds/institutional-characteristics/{year}/ — control, level, religious affiliation
- /ipeds/admissions-enrollment/{year}/ — applications, admissions, enrollments
- /ipeds/admissions-requirements/{year}/ — test score requirements
- /ipeds/academic-year-tuition/{year}/ — tuition and fees
- /ipeds/academic-year-tuition-prof-program/{year}/ — professional degree tuition

===== kapow-piw =====
○ kapow-piw · Implement NCES College Scorecard importer   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: Ketan Gangatirkar · Type: task
Created: 2026-03-09 · Updated: 2026-03-09

DESCRIPTION
Package: tools/importer/nces/

Foundation importer — all other importers depend on NCES institutions existing first.

Fetch side:
- Registers FetchConfig pointing to collegescorecard.ed.gov/data/ CSV ZIP
- `kapow fetch nces` downloads and archives to data/downloads/

Import side (`kapow import nces`):
- fields.go — maps 200+ CSV columns to JSONB keys (demographics, test scores, costs, admissions, programs)
- nces.go — parses CSV from archive, filters (skip non-degree-granting, graduate-only, missing OPE IDs), handles OPE6/OPE8 deduplication (prefer lower unit_id)
- Inserts states into location.state
- Creates institutions with coordinates + nces JSONB
- Creates CORE and ACADEMICS datasets


===== kapow-9y0 =====
○ kapow-9y0 · Favicon scraper tool (tools/favicon/)   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: Ketan Gangatirkar · Type: task
Created: 2026-03-09 · Updated: 2026-03-09

DESCRIPTION
Standalone Go tool that scrapes favicons from ~6,000 college/university websites and saves them as 64x64 PNGs keyed by unit_id.

Institution website URLs come from nces->>'school_url' in search_base.institution (6,038 of 6,040 have URLs).

Resolution strategy (priority order):
1. Parse homepage HTML for <link rel="apple-touch-icon"> / <link rel="icon"> tags
2. Fallback to /favicon.ico
3. Fallback to Google favicon service

Key features:
- Configurable concurrency (default 10 workers)
- Resumable (skips existing files unless --force)
- TLS/HTTP fallback, www/non-www fallback
- Multi-format decode (PNG/JPEG/GIF/ICO/BMP) via disintegration/imaging + golang-ico
- Resize to 64x64 Lanczos, output as PNG

===== kapow-e01 =====
○ kapow-e01 · Implement namematch Tier 2 (fuzzy matching)   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: Ketan Gangatirkar · Type: task
Created: 2026-03-09 · Updated: 2026-03-09

DESCRIPTION
Package: tools/importer/internal/namematch/fuzzy.go

Jaro-Winkler fuzzy string matching for names that Tier 1 (deterministic) can't resolve. Expected to cover ~3-4% more matches.

- Uses adrg/strutil for Jaro-Winkler similarity
- When Tier 1 fails, compute similarity against all institutions (pre-filtered by state when available)
- Match threshold: 0.92
- Return top candidates with confidence scores
- Log unmatched names + fuzzy candidates to a JSON report file for review

Unit test: fuzzy match for misspelled institution name.

===== kapow-edj =====
○ kapow-edj · Urban Institute Education Data Portal integration   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: Ketan Gangatirkar · Type: task
Created: 2026-03-09 · Updated: 2026-03-09

DESCRIPTION
The Urban Institute Education Data Portal (https://educationdata.urban.org/) provides a free REST API with JSON responses, keyed by IPEDS unitid (same as our unit_id). No authentication required. Paginated JSON responses.

Base URL: https://educationdata.urban.org/api/v1/college-university/

This single API replaces or supplements many planned data sources. Pull ALL available data — we'll sort out what to surface later.

### Datasets to fetch (all years available)

**IPEDS (core institutional data):**
- /ipeds/directory/{year}/ — name, address, state, URLs, Carnegie class (1980–2024)
- /ipeds/institutional-characteristics/{year}/ — control, level, religious affiliation
- /ipeds/admissions-enrollment/{year}/ — applications, admissions, enrollments
- /ipeds/admissions-requirements/{year}/ — test score requirements
- /ipeds/academic-year-tuition/{year}/ — tuition and fees
- /ipeds/academic-year-tuition-prof-program/{year}/ — professional degree tuition

===== kapow-piw =====
○ kapow-piw · Implement NCES College Scorecard importer   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: Ketan Gangatirkar · Type: task
Created: 2026-03-09 · Updated: 2026-03-09

DESCRIPTION
Package: tools/importer/nces/

Foundation importer — all other importers depend on NCES institutions existing first.

Fetch side:
- Registers FetchConfig pointing to collegescorecard.ed.gov/data/ CSV ZIP
- `kapow fetch nces` downloads and archives to data/downloads/

Import side (`kapow import nces`):
- fields.go — maps 200+ CSV columns to JSONB keys (demographics, test scores, costs, admissions, programs)
- nces.go — parses CSV from archive, filters (skip non-degree-granting, graduate-only, missing OPE IDs), handles OPE6/OPE8 deduplication (prefer lower unit_id)
- Inserts states into location.state
- Creates institutions with coordinates + nces JSONB
- Creates CORE and ACADEMICS datasets


===== kapow-9y0 =====
○ kapow-9y0 · Favicon scraper tool (tools/favicon/)   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: Ketan Gangatirkar · Type: task
Created: 2026-03-09 · Updated: 2026-03-09

DESCRIPTION
Standalone Go tool that scrapes favicons from ~6,000 college/university websites and saves them as 64x64 PNGs keyed by unit_id.

Institution website URLs come from nces->>'school_url' in search_base.institution (6,038 of 6,040 have URLs).

Resolution strategy (priority order):
1. Parse homepage HTML for <link rel="apple-touch-icon"> / <link rel="icon"> tags
2. Fallback to /favicon.ico
3. Fallback to Google favicon service

Key features:
- Configurable concurrency (default 10 workers)
- Resumable (skips existing files unless --force)
- TLS/HTTP fallback, www/non-www fallback
- Multi-format decode (PNG/JPEG/GIF/ICO/BMP) via disintegration/imaging + golang-ico
- Resize to 64x64 Lanczos, output as PNG

## TASK: kapow-n6ku — Filter non-real institution locations

Filter out non-degree-granting satellite campuses, certificate programs, and community colleges from search results.

### Requirements:
- Apply a permanent WHERE clause filtering out institutions with `nces->>'degrees_awarded_highest'` in (0, 1, 2):
  - 0 = Non-degree-granting (satellite/partnership locations like ASU-Pima)
  - 1 = Certificate only (trade schools)
  - 2 = Associate's only (community colleges)
- Only show institutions with `degrees_awarded_highest` >= 3 (Bachelor's or Graduate)
- This filter is **unconditional** — not exposed as a facet in the UI or API
- Apply at the database/query layer so it affects all search paths (search, export, count, etc.)

### Where to look:
- `api/internal/search/` — the search engine that builds SQL queries
- Look at how the base query is constructed and add the filter there
- The `nces` column is JSONB on the `search_base.institution` table

### Testing:
- Add unit tests for the filter
- Ensure existing tests still pass

### Quality gate:
```bash
make api-test-unit && make api-lint
```

### When done:
Commit and push:
```bash
git push -u origin HEAD
```
Report completion with summary.

### Requirements:

**Enrollment steps**: 0, 1000, 2000, ... 10,000 (by 1,000), then 12,500, 15,000, ... 50,000 (by 2,500), then "Unlimited"
**Cost steps**: 0, 5000, 10,000, ... 30,000 (by $5,000), then 40,000, 50,000, ... 80,000 (by $10,000), then "Unlimited"

- All increment steps, bounds, and labels must be factored into well-named constants/configuration (not inline literals)
- Use Mantine Select component for the dropdowns
- Format numbers with commas (e.g., "12,500")
- Cost values should be prefixed with "$" (e.g., "$5,000")

### Where to look:
- `web/src/components/` — find the facet sidebar and existing range input components

- The dropdowns replace the existing min/max text inputs for these specific field types

- Test the dropdown component renders correctly

### Quality gate:

cd web && pnpm build && pnpm lint && pnpm test

## TASK: kapow-4eyw — Add sort options for list and card view

### Requirements:
Add sort controls to list and card views. A dropdown for sort field plus an ascending/descending toggle.

Sort options:
1. Alphabetical (default: A-Z)
2. Distance (only available if location set; default: nearest)
3. Total enrollment (default: high→low)
4. Acceptance rate (default: low→high)
5. Total price (default: low→high)
6. Ranking blend score (default: high→low)
7. Relevance (only available if search query active; default: most relevant)

Each option has a sensible default direction but user can toggle.

### Where to look:
- `web/src/pages/` — search results page
- `web/src/components/` — existing view components (list view, card view)
- `api/graph/schema/` — check if the GraphQL API already supports sort parameters, or if you need to add them
- `api/internal/search/` — the search engine SQL generation (ORDER BY clause)
- If the API already supports sorting, just add the UI controls. If not, add both API support and UI.

### Important:
- This touches both frontend AND backend. Check what the API supports first.
- Use Mantine components (Select, ActionIcon for toggle)
- Store sort preference in URL params so it's shareable

### Testing:
- Frontend: unit test for the sort control component
- Backend: unit test for ORDER BY generation if adding API changes

### Quality gate:
```bash
make test-standard
```
(Since this touches both Go and web)

### When done:
Commit and push, then report completion.

```

## TASK: kapow-b2n — Implement internal/ranking builder

### Requirements:
Package: `tools/kapow/internal/ranking/`

Shared infrastructure for all 9+ ranking importers.

**Files to create:**
- `builder.go` — Core types and logic:
  - `Opinion` struct: publication slug, category slug, method (rank/score/tier/range), year, unit_id, rank (int), score (float64), tier (string)
  - `RankingBuilder` — accumulates Opinion values, `Flush(ctx, pool)` batch-writes:
    1. Upsert ranking metadata into `search_base.ranking`
    2. Upsert into `search_base.annual_dataset` (theme=RANKING)
    3. Merge into `institution.rankings` JSONB
  - `deriveOpinion()` — parse various rank formats:
    - Numeric ranks: "1", "42"
    - Tier strings: "Tier 1", "Tier 2"
    - Ranges: "201-300" (use midpoint for rank, store range as tier)

- `builder_test.go` — Unit tests:
  - Test `deriveOpinion()` with all format variants
  - Test `RankingBuilder` accumulation logic
  - Integration test for `Flush` (skip when `-short` or no DATABASE_URL)

### Where to look:
- `tools/kapow/internal/` — existing package structure
- `tools/kapow/internal/dbutil/` — database utilities (Connect, etc.)
- `api/migrations/` — look at the schema for `search_base.ranking`, `search_base.annual_dataset`, and `institution.rankings` to understand the table structures
- `tools/kapow/go.mod` — for available dependencies (pgx)

### Important:
- This is a tools/ package, NOT api/ — it lives in the kapow tool module
- Use `$N` parameterized SQL, never fmt.Sprintf for queries
- Pass context as first parameter everywhere
- Use structured logging via slog

### Quality gate:
```bash
cd tools/kapow && go test ./... -short -race && go vet ./...
```

### When done:
Commit and push, then report completion.

## TASK: kapow-e01 — Implement namematch Tier 2 (fuzzy matching)

### Requirements:
Package: `tools/kapow/internal/namematch/`

Jaro-Winkler fuzzy string matching for names that Tier 1 (deterministic normalization) can't resolve.

**Files to create:**
- `fuzzy.go`:
  - `FuzzyMatcher` struct — holds a list of candidate institutions (name, unit_id, state)
  - `NewFuzzyMatcher(candidates []Candidate)` constructor
  - `Match(name string, stateHint string) ([]FuzzyResult, error)` — returns top candidates
  - When Tier 1 fails, compute Jaro-Winkler similarity against all candidates (pre-filtered by state when stateHint is available)
  - Match threshold: 0.92
  - Return top 5 candidates with confidence scores
  - `WriteReport(path string, unmatched []UnmatchedEntry) error` — write JSON report file of unmatched names + their fuzzy candidates

- `fuzzy_test.go`:
  - Test fuzzy match for misspelled institution name
  - Test state filtering reduces candidate set
  - Test threshold filtering (below 0.92 excluded)
  - Test report generation

### Dependencies:
- Use `github.com/adrg/strutil` for Jaro-Winkler similarity (add to go.mod)
- Or implement Jaro-Winkler directly if you prefer (it's ~50 lines)

### Where to look:
- `tools/kapow/internal/namematch/` — may have existing Tier 1 code (normalize.go or similar)
- `tools/kapow/go.mod` — current dependencies
- Check if there's already a `Candidate` or `Institution` type to reuse

### Quality gate:
```bash
cd tools/kapow && go test ./... -short -race && go vet ./...
```

### When done:
Commit and push, then report completion.

## Context
Search results currently include satellite campuses, certificate-only programs, and community colleges. We need to unconditionally exclude institutions where `nces->>'degrees_awarded_highest'` is 0 (non-degree-granting), 1 (certificate only), or 2 (associate's only), keeping only Bachelor's+ (≥3).

## Approach
Add a permanent WHERE clause in `buildWhere()` that is prepended before any user-supplied filters. This is the single function all three query builders (`BuildQuery`, `BuildCountQuery`, `BuildExportQuery`) funnel through, so one change covers all search paths.

## Changes

### 1. `api/internal/search/sql.go` — `buildWhere()`
- Add a hardcoded clause as the first element of `clauses`:
  ```
  COALESCE((data #>> '{nces,degrees_awarded_highest}')::int, 0) >= 3
  ```
- No parameterized args needed — the threshold is a constant, not user input
- `COALESCE(..., 0)` ensures institutions with missing data are also excluded

### 2. `api/internal/search/search_test.go` — unit test
- Add `TestBuildWhere_PermanentDegreeFilter` verifying:
  - The degree filter clause appears in output even with zero user filters
  - The degree filter clause appears alongside user filter clauses
  - Placeholder numbering remains correct (starts at $1 for user filters since the permanent clause uses no placeholders)

## Context

The kapow tool has 9+ ranking importers planned (US News, Forbes, Niche, etc.). Each will parse ranking data from different sources but all need to write to the same three database targets. This package provides the shared infrastructure so individual importers only need to produce `Opinion` structs and call `Flush`.

## Files to Create

### 1. `tools/kapow/internal/ranking/builder.go`

**`Opinion` struct** — one ranking observation:
```go
type Opinion struct {
    PublicationSlug string  // e.g. "us_news"
    CategorySlug   string  // e.g. "national_universities"
    Method         string  // "rank", "score", "tier", "range"
    Year           int
    UnitID         string
    Rank           int     // 0 if not applicable
    Score          float64 // 0 if not applicable
    Tier           string  // "" if not applicable
}
```

**`deriveOpinion(raw string) (method string, rank int, score float64, tier string)`** — parse rank formats:
- Numeric: `"1"`, `"42"` → method="rank", rank=42
- Tier: `"Tier 1"`, `"Tier 2"` → method="tier", tier="Tier 1"
- Range: `"201-300"` → method="range", rank=250 (midpoint), tier="201-300"
- Score-like floats: `"78.5"` → method="score", score=78.5
- Empty/unparseable → error

**`RankingBuilder` struct**:
```go
type RankingBuilder struct {
    opinions []Opinion
    // Publication metadata for upsert into search_base.ranking
    publications map[string]publicationMeta // key = pub_slug + "____" + cat_slug
}

type publicationMeta struct {
    PublicationSlug string
    Publication     string // display name
    CategorySlug    string
    Category        string // display name
    URL             string
}
```

Methods:
- `NewBuilder() *RankingBuilder`
- `SetPublication(slug, name, catSlug, catName, url string)` — register publication metadata
- `Add(op Opinion)` — accumulate an opinion
- `Flush(ctx context.Context, pool *pgxpool.Pool) error` — batch write:
  1. Upsert each unique publication into `search_base.ranking` using `dbutil.BatchExec`
  2. For each opinion, upsert into `search_base.annual_dataset` (theme=`RANKING`, datakey=`pub____cat`)
  3. Group opinions by unit_id, build rankings JSONB map, upsert into `institution.rankings` via `dbutil.UpsertInstitution` (only Rankings field populated)

**Key design decisions:**
- Ranking ID format: `pub_slug + "____" + cat_slug` (matches existing data in DB, e.g. `us_news____national_universities`)
- Theme constant: `"RANKING"` (matches `search_base.theme` enum)
- Use existing `dbutil.UpsertInstitution`, `dbutil.UpsertAnnualDataset`, `dbutil.BatchExec` — no new SQL abstractions needed
- Batch in chunks (e.g. 500 opinions per batch) to avoid memory issues

- `TestRankingBuilderAdd` — verify accumulation, publication registration

**Integration test** (skip when `-short` or no `DATABASE_URL`):

## Context

Enrollment and cost range filters currently use free-text number inputs. Users must type exact numbers, which is slow and error-prone. Replacing these with dropdown selects using predefined increment steps improves usability — users pick from sensible breakpoints instead of guessing values.

## Approach

### 1. Create step configuration module

**New file**: `web/src/components/search/rangeSteps.ts`

Define well-named constants for the dropdown options:

```ts
// Enrollment: 0, 1000, 2000, ... 10000 (by 1,000), then 12500, 15000, ... 50000 (by 2,500)
// Cost: 0, 5000, 10000, ... 30000 (by $5,000), then 40000, 50000, ... 80000 (by $10,000)
```

Exported functions:
- `generateEnrollmentSteps(): number[]` — returns the enrollment breakpoints
- `generateCostSteps(): number[]` — returns the cost breakpoints
- `formatStepLabel(value: number | null, unit: 'enrollment' | 'cost'): string` — formats with commas, `$` prefix for cost, `"No limit"` for null
- `getStepOptions(fieldName: string): SelectOption[] | null` — returns Mantine Select options for fields that should use dropdowns, or `null` for fields that keep text inputs

**Field name mapping** (which fields get dropdowns):
- `enrollment`, `grad_enrollment` → enrollment steps
- `tuition_in_state`, `tuition_out_of_state`, `avg_net_price`, `avg_net_price_0_30k`, `endowment` → cost steps
- Identification: match on `field.name` in the component

The `"No limit"` option uses `null` value (represented as empty string `""` in the Select) for unbounded min/max.

### 2. Modify FilterControl.tsx — NumericalRangeSearchField branch

**File**: `web/src/components/search/FilterControl.tsx` (lines 495–584)

In the `NumericalRangeSearchField` block:
1. Import `getStepOptions` from `rangeSteps.ts`
2. Call `getStepOptions(field.name)` to check if this field uses dropdowns
3. If options exist → render two Mantine `<Select>` components (Min / Max) instead of `<DebouncedNumberInput>`
4. If no options → keep existing text input behavior (no change for percentage fields, etc.)

The Select components:
- Min select: options from first step up to the selected max (or all), plus "No minimum" placeholder
- Max select: options from selected min (or first) to last step, plus "No maximum" placeholder
- Both include "No limit" / unlimited as the unbounded option
- `onChange` converts selected string value back to number and calls `handleRangeChange`
- Clear button remains for resetting both

### 3. Add unit tests

**New file**: `web/src/components/search/rangeSteps.test.ts`

Test cases:
- `generateEnrollmentSteps()` returns correct sequence and length
- `generateCostSteps()` returns correct sequence and length
- `formatStepLabel()` formats enrollment numbers with commas (e.g., "12,500")
- `formatStepLabel()` formats cost with `$` prefix (e.g., "$5,000")
- `getStepOptions()` returns options for enrollment/cost field names
- `getStepOptions()` returns null for non-dropdown fields

## Files to modify

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `web/src/components/search/rangeSteps.ts` | **NEW** — step generation, formatting, option building |
| `web/src/components/search/rangeSteps.test.ts` | **NEW** — unit tests for step logic |
| `web/src/components/search/FilterControl.tsx` | Conditionally render `<Select>` instead of `<DebouncedNumberInput>` for enrollment/cost fields |

## Key decisions

- **Identify fields by `field.name`**, not by `field.unit` — `unit` is `null` for enrollment fields and some cost fields share `DOLLARS` with non-dropdown fields
- **No API changes needed** — the filter value format (`{ min?: number, max?: number }`) stays the same
- **No URL serialization changes** — dropdown values are plain numbers, same as text input
- **Mantine Select** — already a project dependency, consistent with existing UI patterns

cd web && pnpm build && pnpm lint && pnpm test

## Context

Tier 1 name matching (exact, normalized, alias) in `tools/kapow/internal/namematch/` can't resolve misspelled or variant institution names from external data sources. Tier 2 adds Jaro-Winkler fuzzy matching as a fallback for unmatched names.

Types:
- `Candidate` struct: `Name string`, `UnitID string`, `State string`
- `FuzzyResult` struct: `Candidate`, `Score float64`
- `UnmatchedEntry` struct: `Name string`, `StateHint string`, `TopCandidates []FuzzyResult`
- `FuzzyMatcher` struct: holds `[]Candidate`, indexed by state (`map[string][]Candidate`)

Functions:
- `NewFuzzyMatcher(candidates []Candidate) *FuzzyMatcher` — builds state index
- `Match(name string, stateHint string) []FuzzyResult` — normalize input with existing `Normalize()`, pre-filter by state if hint provided, compute Jaro-Winkler similarity against candidates' normalized names, return top 5 above 0.92 threshold sorted by score desc
- `WriteReport(path string, unmatched []UnmatchedEntry) error` — JSON marshal to file

Jaro-Winkler: implement directly (~50 lines) to avoid adding a dependency for one function. Standard algorithm with prefix scaling factor p=0.1, max prefix length 4.

### `tools/kapow/internal/namematch/fuzzy_test.go`

Table-driven tests:
1. Misspelled name matches correct institution (e.g., "Harvrd University" → "Harvard University" with high score)
2. State hint filters candidates (fewer results when state provided)
3. Below-threshold scores excluded (dissimilar name returns empty)
4. WriteReport produces valid JSON file

{"type":"plan_approval_request","from":"koppen-fixer","timestamp":"2026-03-10T23:11:42.183Z","planFilePath":"/home/ketan/.claude/plans/whimsical-noodling-lightning.md","planContent":"# Plan: Köppen Temperature/Precipitation Label Resolution in Exports

## Context

CSV exports show raw letter codes (e.g., "a", "b", "f") instead of human-readable labels ("Hot summer", "Warm summer", "No dry season") for Köppen climate temperature and precipitation subtypes. The climate group field (`koppen_climate`) also has this issue. All three Köppen fields have `EnumOption` mappings defined in the registry (Value→Label), but the export handler ignores them.

## Approach

Add a `LabelForCode` method to `FieldDescriptor` in the search package, then use it in the export handler's `extractCoreOrJSONB` to resolve enum codes to labels. This is a generic solution — any enum field with Options will automatically get label resolution in exports.

## Changes

### 1. `api/internal/search/field.go` — Add `LabelForCode` method

Add to `FieldDescriptor`:

```go
// LabelForCode returns the human-readable label for an enum code.
// If no matching option is found, it returns the code unchanged.
func (d FieldDescriptor) LabelForCode(code string) string {
    for _, o := range d.Options {
        if o.Value == code {
            return o.Label
        }
    }
    return code
}
```

### 2. `api/internal/handler/export.go` — Use label resolution

Change `extractCoreOrJSONB` to accept `FieldDescriptor` (it already does) and resolve enum labels:

```go
func extractCoreOrJSONB(d search.FieldDescriptor, ...) string {
    // ... existing switch for core fields ...
    default:
        if d.OutputKey == "" {
            return ""
        }
        raw := extractJSONBValue(data, d.OutputKey)
        if raw != "" && len(d.Options) > 0 {
            return d.LabelForCode(raw)
        }
        return raw
}
```

### 3. `api/internal/search/field_test.go` — Unit tests for `LabelForCode`

Table-driven test covering:
- Known code → returns label
- Unknown code → returns code unchanged
- Empty options → returns code unchanged
- Empty code → returns empty string

### 4. `api/internal/handler/export_test.go` — Test label resolution in export extraction

Test `extractCoreOrJSONB` with a descriptor that has Options, verifying enum codes are resolved to labels.

## Files to modify

- `api/internal/search/field.go` — add `LabelForCode`
- `api/internal/handler/export.go` — use `LabelForCode` in `extractCoreOrJSONB`
- `api/internal/search/field_test.go` — unit test for `LabelForCode`
- `api/internal/handler/export_test.go` — test label resolution in export path

## Verification

```bash
make api-test-unit && make api-lint
```

message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="sort-options">
{"type":"plan_approval_request","from":"sort-options","timestamp":"2026-03-10T23:12:24.934Z","planFilePath":"/home/ketan/.claude/plans/fancy-mixing-hedgehog.md","planContent":"# Plan: Sort Options for List and Card View (kapow-4eyw)

## Context

Currently, sorting only works in **table view** (via clickable column headers). Card and list views have no sort controls — only a limited "Sort by distance" button. Users need to sort search results in all views by meaningful fields like enrollment, price, acceptance rate, etc.

The backend already fully supports sorting via `SortInput` in the GraphQL API. The URL params `sort` and `sortDir` already drive sort state. This is primarily a **frontend task** with a small backend tweak for blend score sorting.

## Approach

### 1. New `SortControl` component (`web/src/components/SortControl.tsx`)

A dropdown (`Select`) for sort field + an `ActionIcon` for direction toggle. Placed in the results header bar alongside `ViewToggle`, replacing the existing "Sort by distance" button.

**Sort options** (curated list, not all 32 sortable fields):

| Label | Field value | Default dir | Condition |
|---|---|---|---|
| Alphabetical | `name` | ASC | Always |
| Distance | `distance` | ASC | Only when `hasLocation` |
| Total Enrollment | `enrollment` | DESC | Always |
| Acceptance Rate | `admission_rate` | ASC | Always |
| Total Price | `avg_net_price` | ASC | Always |
| Blend Score | `blend_score` | ASC | Only when `hasBlend` |

**Deferred** (needs more backend work):
- **Relevance** — requires passing full-text search terms into the ORDER BY builder as `ts_rank()`. Non-trivial; will be a separate issue.

**Component API:**
```tsx
interface SortControlProps {
  sort: SortState | undefined
  onSortChange: (field: string, direction: 'ASC' | 'DESC') => void
  onSortClear: () => void
  hasLocation: boolean
  hasBlend: boolean
}
```

**Behavior:**
- Selecting a field applies the sort with its default direction
- Clicking the direction toggle flips ASC↔DESC
- Clearing the select removes the sort (back to default name ASC)
- When blend is active and no explicit sort is chosen, show "Blend Score" as the effective sort

### 2. Backend: Allow sort override when blend is active (`api/internal/search/sql.go`)

Currently `sql.go:60-62` forces `ORDER BY 10 ASC NULLS LAST` when blend is set, ignoring user sort. Change to:
- If blend is active AND sort is nil → default to blend score sort (`ORDER BY 10 ASC NULLS LAST`)
- If blend is active AND sort is set → use the user's sort (blend score is still computed as column 10)
- Add `"blend_score"` as a special case in `buildOrderBy` → `ORDER BY 10 <dir> NULLS LAST/FIRST`

Also update `validate.go` to accept `"blend_score"` as a valid sort field when blend is present.

### 3. Frontend: Update `Search.tsx` sort handling

- Remove the "Sort by distance" button
- Add `SortControl` in the results header `<Group>` (between `ViewToggle` and `ColumnPicker`)
- Modify `handleSort` to accept both field and direction (for dropdown use), keeping the existing cycling behavior for table column headers
- Add a new `handleSortSelect` for the dropdown: sets field + default direction
- Stop nullifying sort when blend is active (currently line ~571 clears sort on blend change) — instead, only clear sort if blend is being activated and no explicit sort was set
- Pass sort to the GraphQL query even when blend is active (remove the `hasBlend ? null : sort` guard)

### 4. Frontend: Update GraphQL query variables (`Search.tsx`)

Change line ~449 from:
```tsx
sort: hasBlend ? null : (sort ?? null)
```
to:
```tsx
sort: sort ?? null
```

The backend now handles blend+sort coexistence.

## Files to modify

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `web/src/components/SortControl.tsx` | **New** — sort dropdown + direction toggle |
| `web/src/components/SortControl.test.tsx` | **New** — unit tests |
| `web/src/pages/Search.tsx` | Add SortControl, update sort handlers, remove distance button |
| `api/internal/search/sql.go` | Allow sort when blend active; add blend_score special case |
| `api/internal/search/validate.go` | Accept blend_score sort field when blend present |
| `api/internal/search/sql_test.go` | Test blend_score sort, blend+sort coexistence |

## Verification

1. `make test-standard` — full lint + unit tests for both Go and web
2. Manual: card/list views show sort dropdown; selecting a sort field changes results order
3. Manual: direction toggle flips order; URL params update correctly
4. Manual: blend active → blend score sort available; can still sort by other fields
","requestId":"plan_approval-1773184344934@sort-options@piped-discovering-acorn"}
</teammate-message>

input.prompt:
## WORKTREE SETUP — RUN FIRST
Before doing anything else, create and enter an isolated worktree:
```bash
REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
BRANCH_NAME="worktree/datasources-writer"
WORKTREE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/$BRANCH_NAME"

if [ -d "$WORKTREE_DIR" ]; then
  echo "Worktree already exists, reusing"
else
  git worktree add "$WORKTREE_DIR" -b "$BRANCH_NAME"
fi

cd "$WORKTREE_DIR"
```
Verify you are in the right place:
```bash
echo "CWD: $(pwd)"
echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)"
echo "$(pwd)" | grep -q '\.claude/worktrees/' && echo "WORKTREE VERIFIED" || echo "FATAL: NOT in a worktree"
```
ALL subsequent commands must run from this worktree directory.

## TASK: kapow-4r2 — Write DATA_SOURCES.md

Create `tools/kapow/DATA_SOURCES.md` — comprehensive documentation of every data source for the kapow data pipeline tool.

### For each source, document:
- URL and access method (direct download / API / scrape)
- File format
- Update cadence with expected + by dates
- Manual steps where automation isn't possible
- How to run the fetch command
- Annual import schedule

### Sources to document:
1. **Urban Institute Education Data Portal** — API (covers IPEDS, Scorecard, EADA, Crime, FSA, NACUBO, NHGIS, PSEO). URL: https://educationdata.urban.org/
2. **College Scorecard CSV** — direct download from collegescorecard.ed.gov/data/
3. **Rankings** (each as a subsection):
   - US News (GitHub CSV from frishberg/Archive-of-US-News-College-Rankings)
   - Forbes (API: forbes.com/forbesapi/org/top-colleges/{year}/...)
   - Niche (paginated API scrape from niche.com)
   - Times Higher Education (JSON API or frishberg CSV)
   - QS World (TSV/CSV, also frishberg data)
   - Washington Monthly (JSON/scrape)
   - WSJ (JSON embedded in page)
   - ARWU/Shanghai (scrape from shanghairanking.com)
   - Money (scrape from money.com/best-colleges/)
4. **NCAA Membership Directory** — Playwright scraper
5. **Köppen climate** — public domain ASCII text file
6. **ZIP coordinates** — GeoNames CSV
7. **FiveThirtyEight partisan lean** — GitHub CSV
8. **University hex colors** — GitHub JSON from frishberg/University-Hex-Colors
9. **Application platform membership** — embedded lists (Common App, Coalition, etc.)

### Where to look for context:
- `tools/kapow/` — existing code, main.go, registry.go
- `.beads/issues.jsonl` — issue descriptions contain detailed source info
- `docs/plans/kapow-o0j-data-import-tool.md` — the original data import plan
- Search for issue IDs: kapow-edj, kapow-piw, kapow-yj8, kapow-sgg, kapow-5mj, kapow-xhc, kapow-4er, kapow-qpn, kapow-si8, kapow-zkv, kapow-d7v, kapow-hft, kapow-xtd, kapow-rki, kapow-8hz, kapow-u8c, kapow-ibx

### Quality gate:
No code — just verify the file renders well and covers all sources.

### When done:
Commit and push, then report completion.

input.prompt:
## WORKTREE SETUP — RUN FIRST
Before doing anything else, create and enter an isolated worktree:
```bash
REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
BRANCH_NAME="worktree/mock-auth"
WORKTREE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/$BRANCH_NAME"

if [ -d "$WORKTREE_DIR" ]; then
  echo "Worktree already exists, reusing"
else
  git worktree add "$WORKTREE_DIR" -b "$BRANCH_NAME"
fi

cd "$WORKTREE_DIR"
```
Verify you are in the right place:
```bash
echo "CWD: $(pwd)"
echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)"
echo "$(pwd)" | grep -q '\.claude/worktrees/' && echo "WORKTREE VERIFIED" || echo "FATAL: NOT in a worktree"
```
ALL subsequent commands must run from this worktree directory.

## TASK: kapow-vdg — Mock authentication provider for local development

### Problem:
Local development and E2E testing require either a full Supabase setup or manual JWT generation. This creates friction for new developers and automated tests.

### Requirements:
Add a dev-only mock auth provider that issues valid JWTs without Supabase.

**API side:**
- New endpoint `/dev/auth/login` guarded by `ENV == development` (or similar env check)
- Accepts email, name, and optional sub (user ID) as query params or JSON body
- Returns a signed JWT using the configured `JWT_SECRET` (HS256)
- JWT claims should match the Supabase format (sub, email, aud, exp, etc.)
- Must NOT be accessible in production

**Frontend side:**
- Add a dev-only login UI (e.g., a simple form or button) that appears when the API is in dev mode
- Or a helper function that the demo/E2E tests can call

### Where to look:
- `api/internal/auth/` — existing JWT validation (HMACValidator, RSAValidator)
- `api/internal/middleware/` — auth middleware
- `api/internal/router/` — route registration
- `api/internal/config/` — config struct for environment detection
- `api/cmd/server/main.go` — server startup
- `docs/specs/authentication.md` — JWT claims format and Supabase mapping

### Testing:
- Unit test: the mock endpoint returns a valid JWT that the existing validator accepts
- Unit test: the endpoint returns 404 when not in development mode

### Quality gate:
```bash
make api-test-unit && make api-lint
```

### When done:
Commit and push, then report completion.

**What changed:**
- Added a permanent WHERE clause in `buildWhere()` (`api/internal/search/sql.go`): `COALESCE((data #>> '{nces,degrees_awarded_highest}')::int, 0) >= 3`
- This filters out non-degree-granting (0), certificate-only (1), and associate's-only (2) institutions from all search paths (search, count, export)
- No parameterized args needed — the threshold is a constant
- `COALESCE(..., 0)` ensures institutions with missing data are also excluded

Task complete. Here's what was done:

**Problem**: CSV/PDF exports showed raw letter codes ("a", "b", "f") instead of labels ("Hot summer", "Warm summer", "No dry season") for Köppen climate fields.

**Fix** (3 files, 51 lines added):
1. **`api/internal/search/field.go`** — Added `LabelForCode(code string) string` method on `FieldDescriptor`. Looks up the code in the field's `Options` slice and returns the label; falls back to the raw code if not found.
2. **`api/internal/handler/export.go`** — Updated `extractCoreOrJSONB` to call `d.LabelForCode(raw)` when the descriptor has Options. This is generic — any enum field with Options automatically gets label resolution in exports.
3. **`api/internal/search/field_internal_test.go`** — Added `TestFieldDescriptor_LabelForCode` with table-driven cases: known codes, unknown codes, empty code, empty options.

**Quality gate**: `make api-test-unit` and `make api-lint` both pass clean.

<teammate-message teammate_id="mock-auth">
{"type":"plan_approval_request","from":"mock-auth","timestamp":"2026-03-10T23:46:24.637Z","planFilePath":"/home/ketan/.claude/plans/swirling-enchanting-gizmo.md","planContent":"# Plan: Mock Auth Provider for Local Development

## Context
Local development and E2E testing require either a full Supabase setup or manual JWT generation. A dev-only mock auth endpoint eliminates this friction by issuing valid JWTs using the configured `JWT_SECRET`.

## Implementation

### 1. Create handler: `api/internal/handler/devauth.go`

New handler function `DevLogin(secret string)` that:
- Accepts POST with JSON body: `{ "email": "...", "name": "...", "sub": "..." }`
  - `email` required, `name` optional, `sub` optional (generate UUID if absent)
- Creates JWT claims matching Supabase format:
  - `sub` → user ID (provided or generated deterministic UUID from email)
  - `email` → from request
  - `role` → `"authenticated"`
  - `aud` → `["authenticated"]`
  - `iss` → `"kapow-dev"`
  - `iat` → now, `exp` → now + 24h
  - `user_metadata.full_name` → name if provided
  - `app_metadata.provider` → `"dev"`
- Signs with HS256 using `JWT_SECRET`
- Returns JSON: `{ "access_token": "...", "token_type": "bearer", "expires_in": 86400 }`

### 2. Register route in `api/internal/router/router.go`

Inside `New()`, add a conditional route group:
```go
if deps.Config.Env == "development" {
    r.Post("/dev/auth/login", handler.DevLogin(deps.Config.JWTSecret))
}
```
No auth middleware needed — this is the login endpoint itself. Place it alongside the other route groups.

### 3. Write tests: `api/internal/handler/devauth_test.go`

Unit tests:
- **Happy path**: POST with email → returns valid JWT that `HMACValidator` accepts; verify claims match input
- **With all fields**: POST with email + name + sub → verify all claims populated correctly
- **Missing email**: POST without email → 400 Bad Request
- **Token validates**: Returned token passes through `auth.NewHMACValidator(secret).Validate()`

### 4. Route guard test: `api/internal/router/router_test.go`

- Verify `/dev/auth/login` returns 404 when `Config.Env != "development"`
- This can be a simple test that creates a router with `Env: "production"` and hits the endpoint

## Files to modify/create
- **Create**: `api/internal/handler/devauth.go`
- **Create**: `api/internal/handler/devauth_test.go`
- **Modify**: `api/internal/router/router.go` (add route registration)

## Dependencies to reuse
- `api/internal/auth.Claims` — JWT claims struct (`api/internal/auth/jwt.go`)
- `api/internal/auth.NewHMACValidator` — for test validation (`api/internal/auth/jwt.go`)
- `github.com/golang-jwt/jwt/v5` — already in use for token creation
- `api/internal/config.Config.Env` — environment check (`api/internal/config/config.go`)
- `api/internal/config.Config.JWTSecret` — signing secret

## Verification
```bash
cd /path/to/worktree
make api-test-unit && make api-lint
```
","requestId":"plan_approval-1773186384637@mock-auth@piped-discovering-acorn"}

- Created `api/internal/handler/devauth_test.go` with 7 test cases: happy path, all fields, missing email (400), invalid JSON (400), deterministic sub, audience claim, UUID format

**Quality gate:** `make api-test-unit` and `make api-lint` both pass clean.

Each option has a sensible default direction.
- `web/src/components/search/SortControl.test.tsx` — **New**: 9 unit tests covering rendering, field selection, direction toggle, and conditional option visibility.
- `web/src/components/search/index.ts` — Barrel export for SortControl.
- `web/src/pages/Search.tsx` — Replaced "Sort by distance" button with SortControl. Added `handleSortChange` and `handleSortClear` handlers. Removed sort nullification when blend is active. Sort now passes through to GraphQL even with blend.
- `web/src/pages/Search.test.tsx` — Updated old distance-button test to verify SortControl presence.
